root_text.sn4.8:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.sn4.8:1.3: Atha kho māro pāpimā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavato santike imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:
root_text.sn4.8:2.4: Na hi so nandati yo nirūpadhī”ti.
root_text.sn4.8:3.4: Na hi so socati yo nirūpadhī”ti.
root_text.sn4.8:4.1: Atha kho māro pāpimā “jānāti maṁ bhagavā, jānāti maṁ sugato”ti dukkhī dummano tatthevantaradhāyīti.

translation_text.sn4.8:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.sn4.8:1.3: Then Māra the Wicked went up to the Buddha and spoke this verse in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn4.8:2.3: For attachments are a man’s delight;
translation_text.sn4.8:3.2: Your cattle also bring you sorrow.
translation_text.sn4.8:3.3: For attachments are a man’s sorrow;
translation_text.sn4.8:3.4: without attachments there are no sorrows.”
translation_text.sn4.8:4.1: Then Māra the Wicked, thinking, “The Buddha knows me! The Holy One knows me!” miserable and sad, vanished right there.
